steps.title: Identify the self-interested motive
steps.content: Scheidegger describes the climbers' exaggerated claims as “self-interested” — they personally benefited from falsely portraying edelweiss as dangerous and hard to reach.

steps.title: Match the personal benefit to the answer
steps.content: By claiming edelweiss grew only in steep, dangerous terrain and then reaching them, the climbers could present themselves as brave and strong mountaineers — that's the personal gain described in choice C. The alternatives don't explain a self-interested motive: A and B benefit others (scientists and visitors), while D addresses flower distribution unrelated to the climbers' self-interest. That matches choice C — C is correct.
